name: codegrep_tool
summary: Search file contents by structural code pattern: metavariables (`$NAME`), balanced brackets, and ellipses (`...`, `....`).

Example: `fn $NAME(...)` matches any function and captures the name. Returns one line per match with file, line, column, matched text, and captured metavariable values. Skips `.git`, `target`, `node_modules`, `vendor` automatically.
constraints: Use this when the match must ignore identifier names, whitespace, or argument shape; use `grep_tool` when the match must be byte-for-byte.
Use the `glob` parameter to scope by file type (`*.rs`, `*.ts`); cheaper than walking the whole tree.
Pair with `read_file_tool` once you have a hit to inspect surrounding code.
anti_patterns: To match a literal substring with no structural meaning, use `grep_tool`.
To find files by name, use `glob_tool`.
For regex features (`\d`, `[a-z]`, `?`, `*`), this tool is not the answer.
An empty or whitespace-only `pattern` is an error, not a match-all; pass a concrete substring.
cautions: IMPORTANT: patterns are structural, not regex. Anchors (`^`, `$`), backslash escapes (`\(`, `\.`), and classes (`\d`, `[a-z]`, `?`, `*`) are NOT syntax. Write the literal you want: `eval(`, not `eval\(`; `import os`, not `^import os$`.
IMPORTANT: `$NAME` captures one word token; `$...NAME` captures a span of tokens; reusing the same name back-references the captured text exactly (whitespace included).
IMPORTANT: `...` matches any sequence of tokens at the same bracket nesting level. In singleline mode it stops at newlines; `....` is required to cross them.
output: Plain text. One match per line: `<path>:<line>:<col>: <matched_text>` followed by `[$NAME=value, ...]` when the pattern captured any metavariables.
Example: `src/lib.rs:42:1: fn calculate(items: Vec<i32>) -> i32 { 0 } [$NAME=calculate]`. Newlines inside `<matched_text>` are rendered as `\n`; long matches are truncated.

pattern.description: Codegrep pattern: literal text, balanced brackets, metavariables (`$NAME` for one word, `$...NAME` for a span), and ellipses (`...` for a token sequence, `....` for one that crosses newlines). Must be non-empty.

path.description: Directory or file to search under (default: `.`).

glob.description: File-name filter (e.g. `*.rs`, `*.{ts,tsx}`). Applied before content search.

mode.description: `multiline` (default) treats newlines as whitespace, like a formatter would. `singleline` makes `...` stop at newlines so a pattern stays within one line; use `....` to span lines.

caseless.description: Match identifiers without regard to letter case (default: false).

max_results.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default: 100).
